description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who has been asked to enforce a disk-usage policy “as code”.  
A tiny project workspace has already been provisioned for you under **/home/user/workspace**.  
Your job is to:

1. Analyse the exact on-disk sizes of every regular file stored anywhere beneath **/home/user/workspace** (the directory itself, its immediate files and every nested sub-directory).  
2. Generate a machine-readable usage report called **disk_usage_report.json** inside **/home/user/workspace**.  
3. Evaluate the report against the following policy rule and write a human-readable compliance log called **policy_compliance.log** (also inside **/home/user/workspace**):

   Policy rule:  
   • Any individual file whose size is strictly greater than 3 072 bytes (3 KiB) must be flagged as a warning.  
   • All other files pass.  
   • The script must print a concise summary at the end:  
     “Policy run completed: <warning-count> warnings, <error-count> errors.”

Output file specifications (these formats will be checked byte-for-byte by automated tests, so follow them exactly):

A. /home/user/workspace/disk_usage_report.json  
   • Valid JSON, no extra keys, no trailing commas, pretty-printed with two-space indentation.  
   • Structure:

     {
       "summary": {
         "total_size_bytes": <integer>
       },
       "details": [
         {
           "path": "absolute path as string",
           "size_bytes": <integer>,
           "percentage_of_total": <number with two decimal places>
         },
         …
       ]
     }

   • The order of the objects inside “details” must be ascending by the file’s absolute path.

B. /home/user/workspace/policy_compliance.log  
   • Plain UTF-8 text.  
   • One line per file, following exactly one of the two templates  
     “[OK] <path> size <size_in_bytes>B within 3072B limit”  
     “[WARN] <path> size <size_in_bytes>B exceeds 3072B limit”  
   • After all file lines, append a single summary line:  
     “Policy run completed: <warning-count> warnings, <error-count> errors.”  
   • Ensure every line (including the last) ends with a UNIX newline (LF).

Important guidance:

• Do NOT hard-code the expected sizes; derive them at runtime.  
• Treat symlinks exactly as the file system presents them (the current workspace contains only regular files).  
• Remember to round the “percentage_of_total” field to **exactly two decimal places** (standard rounding) so automated tests match.  
• You do not need root privileges; stay within /home/user.  
• Place both output files directly inside /home/user/workspace.  
• No additional output files should be created.

When you are finished, both prescribed files must exist with the exact contents and formats described above.
truth:
Pre-populated directory tree before the agent starts:

/home/user/workspace
├── file1.txt              (1 024 bytes – created with “dd if=/dev/zero bs=1 count=1024”)
/home/user/workspace/file2.log          (2 048 bytes)
/home/user/workspace/logs
└── archive
    └── file3.gz           (4 096 bytes)

Exact byte counts used for verification:
file1.txt  = 1024  
file2.log  = 2048  
file3.gz   = 4096  
Total      = 7168

The automated checker will confirm:

1. /home/user/workspace/disk_usage_report.json exists and its parsed JSON exactly equals

{
  "summary": {
    "total_size_bytes": 7168
  },
  "details": [
    {
      "path": "/home/user/workspace/file1.txt",
      "size_bytes": 1024,
      "percentage_of_total": 14.29
    },
    {
      "path": "/home/user/workspace/file2.log",
      "size_bytes": 2048,
      "percentage_of_total": 28.57
    },
    {
      "path": "/home/user/workspace/logs/archive/file3.gz",
      "size_bytes": 4096,
      "percentage_of_total": 57.14
    }
  ]
}

2. /home/user/workspace/policy_compliance.log exists and contains exactly:

[OK] /home/user/workspace/file1.txt size 1024B within 3072B limit
[OK] /home/user/workspace/file2.log size 2048B within 3072B limit
[WARN] /home/user/workspace/logs/archive/file3.gz size 4096B exceeds 3072B limit
Policy run completed: 1 warnings, 0 errors.

(each line terminated with LF).

The checker also verifies that no extra files were created inside /home/user/workspace.
